title: Lochnera trichophylla
intro: Lochnera trichophylla, also known as Laggera or Wild chamomile, is a flowering plant native to tropical Africa, commonly used as a medicinal herb. The health benefits of Lochnera trichophylla include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic properties, making it effective in treating digestive issues, fever, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Lochnera trichophylla is commonly prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, particularly digestive issues and fever.

properties: Lochnera trichophylla, also known as creeping Thunbergia, is a plant with reported therapeutic properties. It has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems. Additionally, Lochnera trichophylla is believed to have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, making it a subject of interest in modern research. However, more studies are needed to confirm the plant's efficacy and potential applications in medicine.

preparations: Lochnera trichophylla is also known as creeping thyme, and it is used in herbal preparations for its medicinal properties. The essential oil extracted from its leaves is said to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations made from Lochnera trichophylla are used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems and skin conditions.
